semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all manifest semantic fields, all formal targets, and every sentence surfaced by the conservative prose scanner. Three prose-only invariants remain owned material atoms; the reachable-boundary additions map to the existing formal atoms or to explicit evidence-limit prose and do not create a support transition.

scope.population: Architectures for advanced AI systems that combine multiple cognitive or control responsibilities and may produce consequential external effects.
scope.environment: The ASI Stack reference architecture, its repository artifacts, and controlled executable slices; production and open-world deployments are outside the currently evidenced scope.
scope.model: Substrate-neutral logical architecture in which one model, multiple models, deterministic services, tools, and people may implement roles without one physical module per layer.
scope.authority: Corben Sorenson owns the book's design decision; runtime, institutional, and deployment authority must be supplied by the implementing system and is not created by this chapter.
scope.time: Architecture and comparator review current through 2026-07-14; empirical comparators must be re-frozen before outcome-bearing work.
scope.artifact: chapters/asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.qmd and its manifest, Layer Boundary Record, proof targets, fixtures, and traceability consumers

contrary_evidence: CAIS, MRKL-style systems, LLM-agent decompositions, cognitive architectures, and layered control show that composition and logical roles are established prior-art families rather than proof of this stack's novelty or efficacy.
Embedded-agency analysis shows that explicit interfaces do not place the stack outside the world, eliminate self-reference, or make its world model complete.
The repository has finite records, fixtures, and formal predicates but no deployed end-to-end advanced-AI system demonstrating that the full decomposition survives open-world integration pressure.
known_confounds: Logical responsibility separation can be mistaken for a requirement to deploy one model or process per layer.
A well-documented monolithic implementation may satisfy logical boundaries even when its physical architecture is not modular.
Governance overhead, evaluator quality, task mix, model capability, and implementation maturity can confound any measured advantage attributed to layer separation.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow the claim when only repository traceability, finite handoff denial, or one implementation slice is established rather than the full architecture or efficiency consequence.
Separate logical responsibility boundaries from physical modularity and separate governance adequacy from capability or performance.
refutation_criteria: Refute the exact atom when a reachable counterexample satisfies its assumptions and violates its obligation, or when a competent matched campaign fails its preregistered gate.
Refute any necessity or superiority reading if an undifferentiated comparator supplies the same authority, evidence, rollback, and traceability properties at equal or lower joint cost.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate an atom only when it is duplicated by a more precise canonical owner or superseded by a revised contract and every chapter, proof, test, and reader consumer is migrated.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze the integrated architecture and matched-comparator campaigns after P2/P3 semantics and executable slices exist.

non_claims: Semantic review does not establish proposition truth or promote support.
Logical layers do not require one model, process, service, or physical module per layer.
Finite records and Lean predicates do not establish deployed enforcement, architecture efficiency, open-world safety, AGI, or ASI.

assumptions: The target system has separable responsibilities whose authority and evidence duties can be represented without requiring physical modularity.
Governance, rollback, and epistemic traceability are design objectives in addition to task capability.
The word efficient refers to a joint frontier including quality, safety, latency, cost, governance burden, and residuals rather than token cost alone.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.core.strongest_counterclaim: One undifferentiated model or agent service can internally implement every required authority, evidence, memory, rollback, and routing duty, making an explicit governed-stack specification unnecessary or strictly more costly.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.core.falsifier: A matched, independently observed comparator with no explicit logical layer contracts preserves the same authority ceilings, evidence transitions, rollback closure, residual accounting, and useful-task quality while the stack adds no joint-frontier benefit, or the proposed responsibilities cannot be separated without contradiction.

required_lanes.necessity: Establish the strongest service, agent, cognitive-architecture, layered-control, and embedded-agency comparators and prevent a false novelty claim.
Make the choice to value bounded authority, traceability, reversibility, and residual custody explicit rather than deriving it from performance data.
Check the finite authority, handoff, evidence, and noninterference obligations that make the logical separation meaningful.
Show that the named contracts compose in real governed work, learning, and assurance traces rather than only in a diagram.
Test the efficient qualifier on a joint quality, safety, latency, cost, governance, and residual frontier.
Test whether the logical decomposition survives different models, workloads, and implementation topologies.

acceptance_criteria: The reference architecture assigns every load-bearing responsibility one canonical authority/evidence owner and composes at least the governed-work, learning, and assurance slices without ambiguous ownership.
A preregistered matched comparison shows the explicit stack is not dominated on the declared joint frontier and its claimed governance properties remain observable under transfer.

assumptions: The book intends to present one coherent architecture rather than independent essays.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.problem.001.strongest_counterclaim: A plural anthology with explicit cross-references would serve the intended readers as well as one architecture frame.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.problem.001.falsifier: Reader and architecture audits show that independent chapter frames preserve all required ownership, handoff, and evidence relationships without one shared vocabulary or contract spine.

required_lanes.necessity: This is an editorial coherence requirement chosen by the author, not an empirical architecture result.

acceptance_criteria: Every chapter can be placed in one manifest-ordered contract spine with explicit handoffs and no contradictory ownership.

assumptions: A larger model, prompt wrapper, or agent loop is evaluated by its explicit public contract rather than capabilities that an observer imagines it might contain.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.insufficiency.strongest_counterclaim: Modern compound agents already define authority, memory, evidence, tool permission, and safe replacement sufficiently, so the alleged gap is obsolete.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.insufficiency.falsifier: A current primary-source comparator explicitly specifies and enforces every named boundary—authority, memory discipline, evidence state, permission, replacement, and rollback—without importing an equivalent governed-stack contract.

required_lanes.necessity: The insufficiency claim is comparative and must be tested against current primary descriptions rather than remembered agent stereotypes.
Where the claim concerns enforced rather than declared behavior, reproduce the comparator's relevant boundary or record an exact blocker.

acceptance_criteria: A dated comparator matrix shows each selected model/agent frame omits at least one named boundary at the claimed scope, with no universal claim beyond the reviewed set.

assumptions: The purpose of the minimum is addressability and honest claim ownership, not demonstrated advanced-AI behavior.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.minimum.001.strongest_counterclaim: A stack map, boundary table, source crosswalk, and claim ledger are documentation only and cannot exercise a meaningful architecture boundary.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.minimum.001.falsifier: A new or changed layer cannot be added through the minimum bundle without ambiguous ownership, missing artifacts, silent support movement, or manual changes outside the declared contract.

required_lanes.necessity: Run valid and invalid layer-addition/change cases through the manifest, boundary schema, source crosswalk, and claim ledger.

acceptance_criteria: The minimum bundle accepts one complete layer record and rejects missing owner, authority, artifact, invariant, evidence, or non-claim fields without changing support state.

assumptions: Current comparators can be reproduced or bounded closely enough for joint-frontier comparison.
The mature contracts can be implemented without governance cost overwhelming useful throughput.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.beyond_sota.0.strongest_counterclaim: The mature stack is a repackaging of compound-agent and assurance practices that adds documentation and control overhead without new capability, safety, or governance value.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.beyond_sota.0.falsifier: The exact mature implementation is dominated by a strong matched comparator, fails any required authority/evidence/rollback gate, or does not transfer beyond the tuned implementation.

required_lanes.necessity: Fix the exact current comparator and prevent architecture-brand novelty laundering.
Implement the mature cross-layer contracts and integrated trace kernel.
Measure the complete joint frontier rather than architecture elegance.
Require survival across models, workloads, and implementation topologies.
Expose the value choices behind authority and governance trade-offs.

acceptance_criteria: Every named mature contract exists, has an exercised consumer, survives adversarial controls, and achieves the preregistered exact-dominance or Pareto gate against the dated comparator envelope.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.mechanism.001.proposition: Each governed layer should be specified by a responsibility, interface, artifact, invariant, authority boundary, and failure mode.
assumptions: These fields are sufficient for first-order ownership and can be extended without erasing stable identity.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.mechanism.001.strongest_counterclaim: Some useful responsibilities cannot be localized to one layer or stable interface, so the required fields create false precision.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.mechanism.001.falsifier: A material responsibility repeatedly requires contradictory owners or cannot be represented without hiding shared authority, state, or failure propagation.

required_lanes.necessity: Justify the selected mandatory contract fields and contest omissions.
Exercise complete and incomplete Layer Boundary Records through real consumers.
Check finite ownership, authority, and handoff consistency where representable.

acceptance_criteria: Every active layer has exactly one canonical responsibility owner and consumers reject records missing any required field or carrying contradictory authority.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.mechanism.002.proposition: A raw language model should be treated as a semantic generation and compression component rather than as the authority-bearing whole system.
assumptions: Model outputs are fallible semantic artifacts and external effects require authority not implied by generation ability.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.mechanism.002.strongest_counterclaim: A sufficiently capable model can safely internalize governance and should be treated as the whole system to avoid interface loss and overhead.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.mechanism.002.falsifier: A model-only comparator supplies independently inspectable authority, evidence, rollback, and residual decisions with no separable control boundary, or separating the model makes required behavior strictly less governable.

required_lanes.necessity: Separate capability from permission as a design commitment.
Demonstrate model outputs crossing explicit evidence and action gates.
Measure whether the boundary improves unsafe-release/useful-throughput trade-offs without hiding model capability effects.

acceptance_criteria: Integrated traces show model outputs remain proposals until independent evidence and authority consumers admit them, with matched outcome accounting.

assumptions: The artifact is a single book whose reader promise depends on coherent ownership and handoffs.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.mechanism.003.strongest_counterclaim: A curated anthology can preserve coherence through introductions and cross-references without a single reference architecture.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.mechanism.003.falsifier: The chapter and reader audits preserve all ownership, interface, and evidence relations after the reference-architecture frame is removed.

required_lanes.necessity: This is the author's editorial architecture rule.

acceptance_criteria: Every chapter has a unique architectural job and manifest-ordered handoff rather than project-by-project ownership.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.mechanism.004.proposition: Source queues and evidence states should reduce context omission and unsupported claim strengthening during future writing runs.
assumptions: Writing runs consume the queues and validators rather than bypassing them, and omission/overpromotion can be independently scored.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.mechanism.004.strongest_counterclaim: Queues and evidence labels add maintenance cost but do not reduce omission, contradiction, or support inflation compared with ordinary editing.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.mechanism.004.falsifier: In matched blinded writing tasks, the governed workflow does not improve source coverage or overclaim error within the preregistered uncertainty range, or increases total cost beyond the allowed joint-frontier trade-off.

required_lanes.necessity: Verify that writing runs actually consume queues and evidence-state gates.
Ablate queues and evidence states in matched tasks to estimate their contribution.
Repeat across chapter types and writing agents before claiming general workflow value.

acceptance_criteria: A preregistered matched ablation improves source coverage or reduces unsupported strengthening without unacceptable time, cost, or useful-prose loss.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.interface.001.proposition: Alignment and governance constraints should be explicit inputs to every downstream layer that can affect claims, capability, or external action.
assumptions: Applicable constraints can be represented and propagated, and downstream consumers can fail closed when required constraints are absent.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.interface.001.strongest_counterclaim: Some downstream behavior is too emergent or latency-sensitive for explicit governance inputs, so universal propagation is impossible or harmful.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.interface.001.falsifier: A permitted reachable downstream transition changes a governed claim, capability, or external effect without consuming the applicable constraint and without an explicitly authorized exception.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify constraint propagation and exception semantics over finite traces.
Differentially test runtime consumers against missing, stale, revoked, and conflicting constraints.

acceptance_criteria: All applicable governed transitions carry a current constraint reference or an explicit authorized exception, and mutation controls fail closed.

assumptions: Cross-layer information has schemas or contracts that can preserve identity, provenance, authority, and residuals.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.interface.002.strongest_counterclaim: Hidden shared state or natural-language context is necessary for capable integration and typed artifacts lose meaning at boundaries.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.interface.002.falsifier: A required cross-layer dependency cannot be encoded without losing task-critical semantics, or consumers routinely bypass typed artifacts to achieve valid outcomes.

required_lanes.necessity: Run typed handoffs across the integrated work, learning, and assurance slices with malformed and omitted-field controls.
Check that artifact meaning survives different implementations and model families.

acceptance_criteria: Every cross-layer dependency in the reference slices is carried by a versioned artifact whose producer and consumer agree under positive, negative, and replay tests.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.interface.003.proposition: Recursive improvement should enter as a governed, recorded, reversible transition rather than as ambient self-modification authority.
assumptions: Improvement-relevant state and descendant effects can be enumerated sufficiently to gate promotion and expose residuals.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.interface.003.strongest_counterclaim: Useful recursive improvement requires authority that cannot be fully enumerated or routed through explicit transitions without making it ineffective.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.interface.003.falsifier: A promoted improvement changes governed state or descendants outside the recorded transition and rollback authority, or the explicit route cannot support any useful improvements in an informative regime.

required_lanes.necessity: Model admission, promotion, revocation, descendant invalidation, and rollback obligations.
Exercise full-state update and rollback traces including failure injection.
Measure whether governance changes useful improvement and unsafe promotion outcomes.

acceptance_criteria: Every promoted improvement has a complete authority/evidence transition and effect ledger, and rollback or residual disposition covers all observed state classes.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.invariant.001.proposition: Every active layer boundary should remain explicitly addressable through a stable owner, responsibility, artifact, authority ceiling, invariant set, and handoff.
assumptions: Active layers and handoffs are enumerable at the reference-architecture boundary.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.invariant.001.strongest_counterclaim: Some cross-cutting responsibilities are inherently ambient and cannot have stable addressable boundaries.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.invariant.001.falsifier: An active layer or cross-layer transition lacks a unique addressable boundary while still being admitted as complete.

required_lanes.necessity: Check finite completeness and ownership consistency.
Reject missing and contradictory boundaries in real consumers.

acceptance_criteria: The manifest and runtime/reference records cover every active layer and handoff exactly once at its canonical ownership boundary.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.invariant.002.proposition: Every material claim should retain a stable identity and explicit support state on every public and machine-readable surface that presents it.
assumptions: Material claims and publication surfaces can be enumerated and linked to the claim ledger.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.invariant.002.strongest_counterclaim: Some explanatory prose is too fluid for stable claim identity and support labels without making the book unreadable.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.invariant.002.falsifier: A material claim appears on a governed surface without a resolvable identity/support state, or two surfaces assign incompatible states without an explicit version transition.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify identity and state consistency across finite surfaces.
Scan chapters, appendices, derivatives, and release records for orphaned or contradictory claims.

acceptance_criteria: All adjudicated material claims resolve to one ledger identity and compatible current support state across canonical surfaces.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.invariant.003.proposition: Reasoning or generation capability alone must never grant execution authority.
assumptions: Execution effects and grants are represented separately from model outputs and capabilities.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.invariant.003.strongest_counterclaim: Some low-risk actions should inherit authority directly from sufficiently reliable model confidence to avoid control overhead.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.invariant.003.falsifier: A reachable effect occurs solely because a model generated or scored an action, with no applicable grant, bounded policy exception, or authorized handoff.

required_lanes.necessity: Prove the finite separation between capability output and effect grant.
Inject confident unauthorized proposals and verify that adapters fail closed.

acceptance_criteria: Every observed external effect resolves to a current grant or explicit bounded exception independent of model confidence.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.failure_mode..proposition: Unowned additions can cause anthology drift: content accumulates without a canonical responsibility, artifact, invariant, evidence path, or handoff.
assumptions: Ownership and handoff coherence can be scored before and after additions.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.failure_mode..strongest_counterclaim: Editorial review alone prevents anthology drift without manifest ownership rules.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.failure_mode..falsifier: Across matched additions, removing ownership and handoff requirements does not increase orphan claims, duplicated concepts, contradictory boundaries, or reader discontinuity.

required_lanes.necessity: Detect orphan and duplicate ownership after controlled additions.
Ablate manifest ownership rules in matched update tasks.

acceptance_criteria: Ownership-rule ablation causes a preregistered material increase in orphan, duplicate, contradiction, or continuity failures.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.failure_mode..proposition: Collapsing planning, memory, reasoning, evidence, execution, and governance into ambient model identity can cause monolith drift that hides authority and evidence ownership.
assumptions: Hidden or ambiguous ownership can be observed through handoff, denial, rollback, and evidence queries.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.failure_mode..strongest_counterclaim: A monolithic model can expose equivalent ownership through introspection or one unified policy without logical layer contracts.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.failure_mode..falsifier: A matched monolithic comparator answers all ownership, authority, evidence, rollback, and residual queries with equal accuracy and no explicit logical boundary representation.

required_lanes.necessity: Probe ownership and denial behavior in stack and monolithic implementations.
Ablate explicit boundaries while holding model, task, and information access fixed.

acceptance_criteria: Boundary ablation materially increases ambiguous authority, unverifiable evidence, or incomplete rollback at matched useful throughput.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.failure_mode..proposition: Treating source notes, local tests, formal predicates, or polished prose as broader evidence than their lane permits causes evidence inflation.
assumptions: Claim scope and artifact authority can be independently adjudicated.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.failure_mode..strongest_counterclaim: Ordinary expert editing is enough to prevent evidence overstatement without typed lanes and transition records.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.failure_mode..falsifier: Removing typed evidence lanes and transition gates does not increase independently adjudicated overclaim errors across matched writing and release tasks.

required_lanes.necessity: Detect scope/authority mismatches between claims and artifacts.
Compare matched workflows with and without typed evidence gates.

acceptance_criteria: Gate ablation produces a preregistered material increase in claims whose scope or support exceeds their artifacts.

assumptions: The finite trace enumerates every modeled external-action step, layer authority bit, and authorized handoff relation.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.formal.stack-.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem is vacuous, omits a reachable effect path, or merely restates authorization in its premises.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.formal.stack-.falsifier: A Lean countermodel or runtime refinement trace satisfies the modeled premises while an unauthorized external-action step occurs, or mutation shows an authority/handoff field is semantically unused.

required_lanes.necessity: Kernel-check the exact finite trace property and countermodels.
Refine runtime event traces to the formal states and mutation-test every authority field and consumer.

acceptance_criteria: Lean checks the theorem, nonempty witnesses and unauthorized countercases are reachable, every authority/handoff mutation is rejected, and runtime refinement covers all declared effect paths.

reproduction.evaluator_identity: Lean 4 kernel in the pinned Lake environment

assumptions: Caller ceiling, target requirement, grant state, and handoff decision faithfully represent the admission event.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.formal.stack-.strongest_counterclaim: The rejection theorem follows only because excess authority is encoded as rejection in an assumption or constructor and has no independent consumer consequence.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.formal.stack-.falsifier: A reachable admitted handoff exceeds the caller ceiling, or deleting/mutating the ceiling comparison leaves the theorem and runtime consumer unchanged.

required_lanes.necessity: Check the exact ceiling relation and reachable rejection cases.
Differentially test runtime admission against the formal decision with ceiling, grant, and target mutations.

acceptance_criteria: The theorem rejects reachable excess-ceiling cases, accepts bounded granted cases, and every concrete admission consumer agrees across generated traces.

reproduction.evaluator_identity: Lean 4 kernel in the pinned Lake environment

assumptions: The finite record fields cover the declared admission contract but do not claim completeness for unmodeled open-world effects.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.formal.stack-.strongest_counterclaim: The route theorem is a field-completeness projection that provides traceability but no semantic guarantee that values are true, enforced, or sufficient.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.formal.stack-.falsifier: A required field can be removed or corrupted without changing the route, or an admitted record fails a declared concrete consumer despite all modeled completeness predicates.

required_lanes.necessity: Check deterministic routing for each exact missing-field class and nonempty valid records.
Mutation-test serialized fields and all consuming validators/admission gates; do not treat schema completeness as safety.

acceptance_criteria: Every required-field mutation routes to its exact rejection, complete records reach bounded admission, and runtime consumers refine the same decisions without overclaiming value truth or deployed enforcement.

reproduction.evaluator_identity: Lean 4 kernel in the pinned Lake environment

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.e0bd068.proposition: Every high-impact component should expose behavior, authority, evidence, and rollback-relevant state to an observer that is not the component itself.
assumptions: High impact is prospectively defined and an observer can be isolated sufficiently to detect omissions without claiming full independence.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.e0bd068.strongest_counterclaim: Some capable components are inherently opaque and can be governed adequately through input/output tests alone.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.e0bd068.falsifier: A high-impact component with no external observability meets every declared authority, evidence, rollback, and failure-detection gate, or the proposed observer cannot detect seeded omissions above chance.

required_lanes.necessity: Define which impacts justify inspection and the privacy/security limits on observers.
Seed hidden authority, evidence, and rollback defects and test external detection.
Measure detection error, latency, cost, and false refusal against black-box alternatives.

acceptance_criteria: The external observer detects preregistered material defect classes at the required error rate without unacceptable useful-throughput or privacy cost.

assumptions: Logical responsibility and authority can be specified independently of deployment topology.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.2cb1eb1.strongest_counterclaim: Calling a layer logical makes the architecture unfalsifiable because any implementation can be redescribed post hoc as layered.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.2cb1eb1.falsifier: The architecture cannot give prospective observable criteria for assigning a responsibility/authority boundary before inspecting outcomes, or two incompatible assignments are equally valid with different governance consequences.

required_lanes.necessity: Define the abstraction boundary and prevent physical-module or post-hoc relabeling errors.
Apply the same prospective layer criteria to monolithic, modular, and hybrid implementations.

acceptance_criteria: Independent implementations receive stable layer assignments from responsibility, artifact, authority, and consumer criteria without requiring one physical module per layer.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.55051a3.proposition: A handoff whose consumer cannot identify its required input artifact or authority ceiling should fail closed with an explicit missing-contract record.
assumptions: The consumer declares which artifact and authority fields are required for the attempted operation.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.55051a3.strongest_counterclaim: Safe defaults or model judgment can often infer missing artifacts and authority without blocking useful work.
asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.55051a3.falsifier: A governed consumer proceeds with the effect despite absent required artifact identity or authority ceiling, or fail-closed behavior causes unacceptable false refusal in the preregistered valid-missing-metadata class.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify missing-contract and explicitly authorized default semantics.
Inject missing, stale, conflicting, and valid-default cases into real consumers.
Measure false refusal and unsafe inference rates where defaults are proposed.

acceptance_criteria: All required-field omissions fail closed or use a prospectively authorized bounded default, with unsafe inference and false-refusal rates inside frozen limits.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.1f0290b.rationale: Reader-path summary of already owned chapter and downstream claims; it adds no independently testable proposition.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.74a7534.rationale: Restates the governed-stack core and layer-contract mechanism.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.e0bd068.rationale: Adds a distinct external-observability requirement for high-impact components.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.0e1cd7f.rationale: Restates the mandatory Layer Boundary Record fields.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.839c2c1.rationale: Restates the core contrast with model-, loop-, and benchmark-only descriptions.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.de4130a.rationale: Restates the operational admission criteria for a layer contract.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.9233fb8.rationale: Applies the book-as-reference-architecture editorial rule to novelty ownership.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.3258c1d.rationale: Owned more precisely by the Virtual Context ABI insufficiency claim about retrieval without authority, bindings, adequacy, and typed failure.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.21db0e0.rationale: Owned by the benchmark-ratchet insufficiency and anti-Goodhart failure program.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.c47a09f.rationale: Concrete example of the model/agent insufficiency proposition.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.073c75d.rationale: Restates the governed-stack core separation of model capability from constitutional, memory, deployment, approval, and evidence authority.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.2cb1eb1.rationale: Defines the substrate-neutral meaning of layer and blocks a physical-module misreading.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.91d0b0e.rationale: Comparator summary whose truth is governed by the cited source notes, not a local architecture result.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.7cdc971.rationale: Expanded description of the Layer Boundary Record and minimum implementation.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.4ac9416.rationale: Restates separation between model output, evidence admission, and execution authority.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.3230238.rationale: Owned by the living-book synchronization and validation mechanism.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.f258520.rationale: Restates stable layer boundary addressability under implementation change.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.55051a3.rationale: Adds a concrete fail-closed handoff behavior for missing input or authority contracts.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.42f477c.rationale: Applies the book-as-reference-architecture ownership rule to chapter insertion.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.8a22bd4.rationale: Editorial metaphor summarizing the reference-architecture rule rather than adding a separate proposition.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.b951834.rationale: Owned by the living-book synchronized-update mechanism.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.bd8fb9e.rationale: Concrete instance of the model-only insufficiency claim.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.30bd19f.rationale: Concrete modular-system instance of the same governance-contract insufficiency claim.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.1819a04.rationale: Restates the mandatory layer-contract mechanism with comparator context.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.226f593.rationale: Elaborates the typed-artifact interface by contrasting hidden state.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.369689b.rationale: Current support-state and promotion-burden boundary for the core atom, not an additional architecture claim.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.0dbff04.rationale: Transition sentence into measurement and the next chapter; no independently testable content.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.f4b2bd9.rationale: Owned by the next chapter's efficient-ASI core claim.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.b81edb0.rationale: Fragment of the embedded-agency limitation paragraph; the full proposition is governed by the cited source and existing non-claim boundary.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.941a90f.rationale: Restates the within-ceiling grant and dispatch-custody obligation already owned by the operational formal target.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.37b2091.rationale: Reports the bounded Lean consequences already owned by the two layer-boundary formal atoms.

asi-is-a-stack-not-a-model.prose.912aba3.rationale: Exact non-claim boundary for the new finite consumer; it constrains interpretation and creates no positive support claim.
